purpose: Public-shape SoT for the free-tier email opt-in endpoint added by POWER-USER-OUTREACH-W1-V2. Future modifications to this endpoint ship a NEW dated snapshot (never mutate this one).

email.constraints: RFC-5321 max length 254; matches EMAIL_RE; lower-cased server-side

source.note: Unknown values silently default to welcome-paywall

success_envelope.inserted: <boolean — true on first opt-in, false on repeat>

rate_limit.current: express-rate-limit at app-level (5/min/IP default per the existing global middleware)
rate_limit.future: Per-endpoint limiter (10/hr/IP) flagged as follow-up if abuse surfaces

side_effects: INSERT (or UPDATE on conflict) into signup_emails postgres table
Fire-and-forget UPDATE signup_emails.confirmation_sent_at on Resend success
Console log success / failure (no PII in success log — email masked)

idempotency.guarantee: At-most-once confirmation-email send per opt-in event (event_id = signup-email:<sha256(email)>:<unix-second>)
idempotency.behavior_on_retry: Repeat POST within the same second returns ok:true but skips re-sending the email (claim returns false). Repeat POST hours/days later is a fresh event (caller protection only for burst retries).

drift_check_command: curl -fsS -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"email":"delivered@resend.dev","source":"welcome-paywall","optin_consent":true}' https://api.algovault.com/api/signup-email | jq -e '.ok == true and (.optin_at | test("^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T")) and (keys | inside(["ok","optin_at","inserted","error"]))' >/dev/null && echo DRIFT_CHECK_OK || echo DRIFT_CHECK_FAIL
